jobs.basic_qualifications:
- Bachelor's degree holder
- Proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite and related applications
- Advanced English proficiency (B2 or equivalent) in both verbal and written communication
- Advanced Japanese proficiency (N2 or equivalent) in both verbal and written communication
jobs.description:
Note:
•Fully Remote setup but candidate must be physically based in Metro Manila Cavite, Laguna, Rizal or Bulacan
•Advanced Japanese proficiency - minimal JLPT N2
•Minimum a Bachelor Degree required


The Global Solutions and Risk Compliance (GSRC) organization is a component of Amazon's operations, dedicated to ensuring the compliance of its products and services worldwide. GSRC's core mission is to keep customers, partners, and associates safe while protecting the company from legal and regulatory risks. With a diverse global team spanning across India, China, Poland, Romania, Netherlands, Jordan, Philippines and Costa Rica, GSRC partners with its stakeholders to classify products and ensure regulatory and legal compliance for product listings. GSRC raises the bar on quality by implementing operational changes matching the everchanging regulatory space. The team designs global, consistent, and scalable solutions and prioritize a blend between technology-driven and human decision-making processes. GSRC believes that innovation thrives on diversity, fostering an inclusive and equitable culture that empowers employees with opportunities for learning and development

Key job responsibilities
As a Senior Compliance Associate, you will review product information against regulatory documentation to ensure compliance for Amazon listings. Using Amazon tools, you will classify products and address deviations from SOPs in collaboration with your manager and experts. You will prioritize tasks using reports to meet quality, productivity, and service targets. You may identify improvement opportunities, like automating reports with Excel, and contribute to SOP refinement. You will share expertise in knowledge-sharing sessions and mentor peers.

Required Skills:
- Proficient in Microsoft Excel (data manipulation, pivot tables, etc.
- Basic SQL knowledge for data queries
- Experience with CRM tools
- Strong attention to detail and data analysis skills
- Problem-solving abilities with logical and creative approaches
- Commitment to research and data interpretation.
jobs.description_short: Note: •Fully Remote setup but candidate must be physically based in Metro Manila Cavite, Laguna, Rizal or Bulacan•Advanced Japanese proficiency - minimal JLPT N2 •Minimum a Bachelor Degree requiredThe
jobs.preferred_qualifications:
- Strong knowledge of consumer product compliance processes and regulations
- Proven experience in regulatory compliance management, including working with government agencies
- 2+ years experience in Business Process Outsourcing across domains such as accounting, regulatory compliance, payment processing, IT services, human resources, or quality assurance
- Advanced proficiency in Excel, including VBA
- Expertise in database querying (SQL)
- Experience as a quality auditor in retail chain management, Fast-Moving Consumer Goods (FMCG) companies, or corporate environments
- Background in data analysis within quantitative fields, including the development of data and decision support systems

jobs.title: Product Compliance Associate (Remote, Japanese), Global Solutions and Risk Compliance (GSRC)

jobs.basic_qualifications:
- 3+ years of vendor management experience
- 4+ years of relevant work in a data center or other critical environment experience
- 5+ years of data center engineering or operations experience
- Bachelor's degree in Electrical Engineering, Mechanical Engineering, or a related field
jobs.description:
AWS Infrastructure Services owns the design, planning, delivery, and operation of all AWS global infrastructure. In other words, we’re the people who keep the cloud running. We support all AWS data centers and all of the servers, storage, networking, power, and cooling equipment that ensure our customers have continual access to the innovation they rely on. We work on the most challenging problems, with thousands of variables impacting the supply chain — and we’re looking for talented people who want to help.

You’ll join a diverse team of software, hardware, and network engineers, supply chain specialists, security experts, operations managers, and other vital roles. You’ll collaborate with people across AWS to help us deliver the highest standards for safety and security while providing seemingly infinite capacity at the lowest possible cost for our customers. And you’ll experience an inclusive culture that welcomes bold ideas and empowers you to own them to completion.

The Data Center Capacity Delivery Asia-Pacific Controls Deployment and Service (DCCD APAC CDS) team delivers and services the control systems which monitor and ensure the health of our data centers. APAC CDS’ mission is to safely provide our customers with the unmatched reliability, security, and performance that AWS is known for. APAC CDS covers six disciplines of deployments, projects, service, colo, on-call support, and management.

Controls Deployment & Service (CDS) Engineers deliver and support data center building automation systems, such as Building Management Systems (BMS) and Electrical Power Monitoring Systems (EPMS). CDS Engineers work to deploy and service building automation systems, to ensure the health of our data centers. As a CDS Engineer, you may oversee deployment of a new BMS or EPMS as part of a new data center build, deliver projects to upgrade an existing data center owned or leased by Amazon, or deliver projects to upgrade collocated data centers with Amazon hardware. As a CDS Engineer, you may also be required to provide local servicing expertise to our fleet or data centers in your region, and occasionally assist with global support. Successfully working as a CDS Engineer will also require you to partner effectively with the many teams at Amazon that make our data center deployments and operations a success, including Construction, Commissioning, Critical Projects, Data Center Operations, Procurement, Program Management, Design, and Engineering teams, as well as maintaining strong relationships with our vendors.

As Amazonians, we work in a competitive environment. Our customers are always beautifully, wonderfully dissatisfied. Being successful in our roles is not just about doing our jobs well, but also evolving and innovating. Change is the only constant, and applies to our customers, our roles, our team organization, our tools, and our mechanisms.

To be successful in this team, you will also have:
- Ability to thrive at the pace and scale of Amazon
- Ability to work independently and manage multiple competing priorities
- Ability and willingness to collaborate with peers around the globe, which may involve some participation in meetings at odd hours
- Ability and willingness to travel domestically or globally as and when needed


Key job responsibilities
- Deployment of data center controls, on time, with effective management of vendors to ensure minimal defects at the point of Power and Facilities Hand-Off (PFHO)
- Delivery of critical projects to upgrade data center automation
- Service and support provided to partner teams, providing diagnosis and resolution of Loss of Visibility (LOV), Loss of Resilience (LOR), Large Scale Event (LSE), Critical Scale Event (CSE), and other operations impacting events
- Resolution of tasks within predefined Service Level Agreements (SLAs)
- Delivery of further initiatives to improve the efficiency of our data centers or our operations procedures


About the team
About AWS
Diverse Experiences
Amazon values diverse experiences. Even if you do not meet all of the preferred qualifications and skills listed in the job description, we encourage candidates to apply. If your career is just starting, hasn’t followed a traditional path, or includes alternative experiences, don’t let it stop you from applying.

Why AWS
Amazon Web Services (AWS) is the world’s most comprehensive and broadly adopted cloud platform. We pioneered cloud computing and never stopped innovating — that’s why customers from the most successful startups to Global 500 companies trust our robust suite of products and services to power their businesses.

Work/Life Balance
We value work-life harmony. Achieving success at work should never come at the expense of sacrifices at home, which is why we strive for flexibility as part of our working culture. When we feel supported in the workplace and at home, there’s nothing we can’t achieve.

Inclusive Team Culture
AWS values curiosity and connection. Our employee-led and company-sponsored affinity groups promote inclusion and empower our people to take pride in what makes us unique. Our inclusion events foster stronger, more collaborative teams. Our continual innovation is fueled by the bold ideas, fresh perspectives, and passionate voices our teams bring to everything we do.

Mentorship and Career Growth
We’re continuously raising our performance bar as we strive to become Earth’s Best Employer. That’s why you’ll find endless knowledge-sharing, mentorship and other career-advancing resources here to help you develop into a better-rounded professional.
jobs.description_short: AWS Infrastructure Services owns the design, planning, delivery, and operation of all AWS global infrastructure. In other words, we’re the people who keep the cloud running. We support all AWS data centers

jobs.basic_qualifications:
- - Bachelor’s degree or equivalent business school education.
- - Excellent written and verbal communication skills in Japanese; good written English skills.
- - Strong presentation and communication abilities, able to express thoughts logically and succinctly; excellent listening skills, highly consultative and solutions-oriented.
- - Data analysis skills — ability to collect, clean, interpret, challenge, and analyze data and provide actionable solutions; demonstrated ability to manage business operations.
- - Influencing and metrics management skills — ability to understand business metrics and create realistic forecasts; strong negotiation skills.
- - Ability to operate independently in a fast-paced, sometimes ambiguous environment, with full responsibility for achieving business goals.
- - Ability to develop original ideas, approaches, and solutions for typical and complex issues.
- - Ability to work collaboratively within teams, focusing on delivering high-quality results.
- - Proficient in Microsoft Office applications (Excel, Outlook, Word) and content management systems; quick to learn and use new tools and systems.
- - Ability to manage and drive business outcomes independently in a fast-changing, sometimes uncertain environment.
jobs.description:
Amazon is the most customer-centric company. Work hard. Have fun. Make history.
With this philosophy in mind, Amazon is committed to providing the highest level of service to our most engaged customers — including vendors, sellers, and beyond — encouraging them to grow their businesses on the Amazon platform. At Amazon, we expect no more and no less from you.
Since Amazon opened its virtual doors, our goal has always been to become the most customer-centric company in the world. How do we achieve this? By having people like you who ensure that customers can find everything they’re looking for online — at great prices and with convenience.
Both Amazon and the vendors and sellers who list their products on our sites now offer millions of new and used items across a wide range of categories. Join a company that reinvents itself every day. Immerse yourself in a fast-paced, constantly expanding environment, continuously innovating new efficiencies and offering great opportunities for both our customers and you.
You will help Amazon create the Earth’s greatest shopping experience — a store offering more products, more brands, and more compelling content than any other online or offline store. This is an excellent platform to launch your career with Amazon.
________________________________________
As an Account Manager, You are expected to be fluent in Japanese and preferably well-versed in Japanese business etiquette, as you will be directly supporting vendors in the JP retail market. You will serve directly with vendors and manage aspects of their businesses on the Amazon platform. Through in-depth data analysis and market trend insights, you will assist vendors in developing practical and effective business strategies, driving business growth, and achieving revenue goals, while continuously optimizing key manufacturers’ (vendors') overall performance on the Amazon platform.
Your core responsibilities will focus on day-to-day communication with vendors, strategy formulation, business reporting, and relationship maintenance, ensuring that vendor needs are promptly responded to, solutions are efficiently executed, and long-term stable cooperation is achieved.

Key job responsibilities
- Continuously monitor daily operational data, proactively identify potential risks, ensure the effective execution of strategies, and make timely adjustments as necessary to maintain smooth business operations.
- Based on data, propose feasible optimization solutions to improve the vendor’s performance in supply chain efficiency, traffic acquisition, conversion rates, etc., thereby driving overall sales growth.
- Maintain frequent and high-quality communication with vendors, deeply understand their business needs and concerns, ensure issues are promptly responded to and effectively resolved, and build strong trust and collaborative relationships.
- Provide regular professional and systematic business reports to vendors, clearly communicate goal progress based on quantifiable data, outline future strategic directions and key initiatives, and analyze the core reasons and business implications behind the data.
- Work with vendors to define and achieve business targets.
- Provide weekly reports to relevant internal teams to ensure transparency and promote cross-team collaboration.
- Collaborate closely with internal functional teams, actively coordinate resources, and secure necessary support to help vendors achieve sustainable business growth.
jobs.description_short: Amazon is the most customer-centric company. Work hard. Have fun. Make history.With this philosophy in mind, Amazon is committed to providing the highest level of service to our most engaged customers
jobs.preferred_qualifications:
- - At least 3 years of relevant experience, with experience as an Account Manager preferred.
- - Study experience in Japan preferred.
- - Experience working in a Japanese local company preferred.
- - Knowledge of SQL and Python preferred.

jobs.title: AVS Brand Specialist- JP

jobs.basic_qualifications:
- 7+ years of engineering experience
- 3+ years of engineering team management experience
- 8+ years of leading the definition and development of multi tier web services experience
- Knowledge of engineering practices and patterns for the full software/hardware/networks development life cycle, including coding standards, code reviews, source control management, build processes, testing, certification, and livesite operations
- Experience partnering with product or program management teams
- Experience designing or architecting (design patterns, reliability and scaling) of new and existing systems
- Bachelor's degree in computer science or equivalent
jobs.description:
Lead engineering efforts to support the mission of expanding access to Amazon’s Store by developing inclusive payment solutions for underserved customer groups. Partner with cross-functional teams to define and execute technical strategy for programs such as EBT, healthcare benefit integration, and AI-driven personalization, with a focus on delivering meaningful customer impact.

Amazon Access is focused on reducing barriers and making shopping more accessible and affordable for all customers, guided by the principle that “Success and scale bring broad responsibility.” In the role of Software Development Manager III, you will independently lead software teams responsible for building scalable technical foundations that enable millions of U.S. households — including low-income families, health benefit account holders, and Medicaid recipients — to use their benefits seamlessly on Amazon.

You will own the technical vision and execution across three strategic priorities:

Advancing growth through AI-enhanced personalization,

Broadening access via vendor partnerships and external system integrations,

And evolving core architecture to support long-term scale.

Your team will deliver technologies spanning payments infrastructure, classification systems, and customer experience enhancements that positively affect millions of customers who experience real challenges accessing e-commerce services.

Key job responsibilities
Strategic Technical Leadership

Establish and drive the technical roadmap for key initiatives that enhance accessible payment solutions and customer experiences.

Provide leadership and direction to software engineers working on personalized AI experiences, payment integration projects, and core architecture improvements.

Promote productivity and system efficiency by encouraging the thoughtful adoption of generative AI and simplifying complex system architectures.

Collaborate closely with senior technical leaders to build consensus and alignment around the technical vision and strategic priorities.

Cross-Functional Partnership

Work with product management using a customer-centric approach to translate user needs into effective technical solutions.

Partner with marketing teams to support AI-informed campaign automation and experiences that reflect customer lifecycle insights.

Engage with cross-organizational partners including Payments, Worldwide Grocery, Prime, and Health teams to deliver coordinated technical initiatives and integrated solutions.

About the team
Amazon Access (AA) is transforming the Amazon Store into the ‘Everyone Store’, embodying our belief that 'Success and Scale Bring Broad Responsibility.' Our vision is to create products and services that provide convenience equity, breaking down barriers to make shopping more accessible and affordable for all customers. By enabling government, healthcare and private benefits, we can reach emerging segments and expand the spending of existing Amazon’s Stores customers.

jobs.preferred_qualifications:

- Experience in recruiting, hiring, mentoring/coaching and managing teams of Software Engineers to improve their skills, and make them more effective, product software engineers

jobs.description:
Alexa+ is our next-generation assistant powered by generative AI. Alexa+ is more conversational, smarter, personalized, and gets things done.

Our goal is make Alexa+ an instantly familiar personal assistant that is always ready to help or entertain on any device. At the core of this vision is 'Alexa AI Developer Tech', a close-knit team that’s dedicated to providing software developers with the tools, primitives, and services they need to easily create engaging customer experiences that expand the wealth of information, products and services available on Alexa+.

We build and scale Alexa's core infrastructure that powers millions of customer interactions with sub ms latency. Our team owns mission-critical services including developer artifacts storage, experience lifecycle management, and account linking orchestration. Work on distributed systems that directly impact every Alexa experience and developer worldwide, while solving complex engineering challenges at Amazon scale.

You will join a growing organization working on top technology using Generative AI and have an enormous opportunity to make an impact on the design, architecture, and implementation of products used every day, by people you know.

We’re working hard, having fun, and making history; come join us!


Key job responsibilities
Responsibilities

Responsible for the development and maintenance of key system features that support LLM based experiences.
Will work with other team members to collabarte and align on design approaches, prototype new technology and evaluate technical feasibility.
Will work in an Agile/Scrum environment to deliver high quality software against tight schedules.
Will establish architectural principles, select design patterns and then mentor team members on their appropriate application
Deep dive into the end-to-end system architecture and drive long-term alignment with partner teams.

jobs.description:
The Sales Trainer and Coach is responsible for delivering and facilitating Executive Foundation training, deal coaching, Generative AI enablement, and other worldwide sales training programs. This role also encompasses developing and executing AWS' global curriculum strategy that aligns with customer needs and business outcomes. The position requires program development expertise, cross-functional collaboration with global teams, and strong project management capabilities.


A day in the life
The trainer will be delivering trainings for 1-2 weeks/month, travel within Asia Pacific and Worldwide according to the projects that are running, and coaching Sales or Sales Managers when necessary.

About the team
The team started 7+ years ago and it started of from Onboarding the Sales People. Now we have expanded to Ever-Boarding, and supporting all the leaders and managers in AWS Japan. We are part of Worldwide & APJ team and the business is dynamic. Once you are onboarded you will have a mentor for 3 months who will support your ramp up with your manager.

jobs.description:
AWS Infrastructure Services owns the design, planning, delivery, and operation of all AWS global infrastructure. In other words, we’re the people who keep the cloud running. We support all AWS data centers and all of the servers, storage, networking, power, and cooling equipment that ensure our customers have continual access to the innovation they rely on. We work on the most challenging problems, with thousands of variables impacting the supply chain — and we’re looking for talented people who want to help.

You’ll join a diverse team of software, hardware, and network engineers, supply chain specialists, security experts, operations managers, and other vital roles. You’ll collaborate with people across AWS to help us deliver the highest standards for safety and security while providing seemingly infinite capacity at the lowest possible cost for our customers. And you’ll experience an inclusive culture that welcomes bold ideas and empowers you to own them to completion.

We are seeking a Business Intelligence Engineer professional in Seattle, WA who can operate with a startup mentality while partnering with managers and business leaders to deliver sound, data-backed recommendations and insights, identify areas of improvement and suggest and implement solutions.

The ideal candidate will be highly analytical, excited about data mining and generate insights that will influence the direction the organization. You will be curious and comfortable digging deep into data and developing core metrics to identify areas of improvement and partner with the business to find effective solutions. You will develop new ways to measure and track our performance, and improve the customer experience by building robust technical and analytical solutions to help us better understand customer needs. You will empower data driven decision making through implementing principles of data mining, data modelling, and analytical skills to define and measure metrics for evaluating health of the business. You will be able to extract, integrate and work on critical data to build out data pipelines, automate reports and dashboards to communicate your findings and leverage self-service tools to internal stakeholders.

Key job responsibilities
• Design, develop, implement, test, document, and operate large-scale, high-volume, high-performance data structures for business intelligence analytics.
• Implementing data structures using best practices in data modeling to provide on-line reporting and analysis using business intelligence tools and a logical abstraction layer against large, multi-dimensional datasets and multiple sources.
• Gathering business and functional requirements and translating the gathered requirements into robust, scalable, operable solutions that work well within the overall data architecture.
• Analyzing source data systems and driving best practices in source teams.
• Analyzing large amounts of data, discovering and solving real world problems, and building metrics and business cases to help business teams to make decisions.
• Participating in the full development life cycle, end-to-end, from design, implementation and testing, to documentation, delivery, support, and maintenance to produce comprehensive, usable dataset documentation and metadata.
• Evaluating and making decisions around dataset implementations designed and proposed by peer data engineers. Identifying business process improvements from the data level.
• Analyzing and evaluating business process, and through data driven insights proposing and implementing business process improvement.

About the team
Diverse Experiences
AWS values diverse experiences. Even if you do not meet all of the preferred qualifications and skills listed in the job description, we encourage candidates to apply. If your career is just starting, hasn’t followed a traditional path, or includes alternative experiences, don’t let it stop you from applying.

Why AWS?
Amazon Web Services (AWS) is the world’s most comprehensive and broadly adopted cloud platform. We pioneered cloud computing and never stopped innovating — that’s why customers from the most successful startups to Global 500 companies trust our robust suite of products and services to power their businesses.

Inclusive Team Culture
AWS values curiosity and connection. Our employee-led and company-sponsored affinity groups promote inclusion and empower our people to take pride in what makes us unique. Our inclusion events foster stronger, more collaborative teams. Our continual innovation is fueled by the bold ideas, fresh perspectives, and passionate voices our teams bring to everything we do.

Mentorship & Career Growth
We’re continuously raising our performance bar as we strive to become Earth’s Best Employer. That’s why you’ll find endless knowledge-sharing, mentorship and other career-advancing resources here to help you develop into a better-rounded professional.

Work/Life Balance
We value work-life harmony. Achieving success at work should never come at the expense of sacrifices at home, which is why we strive for flexibility as part of our working culture. When we feel supported in the workplace and at home, there’s nothing we can’t achieve.
